mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns during early-cycle expansion phases, with mean return < 0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and restrictive policy rates create stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of CPIAUCSL > 195 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5 yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% is followed by negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises in same month
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive returns occurring in > 60% of triggered instances.
